findings.description: list.pop(0) inside the prune loop is O(n) per pop (shifts every remaining element), making each check() O(k*n) on the hottest per-request path; replace the per-key list with collections.deque and use popleft() for O(1) pruning.

self._hits[key] on a defaultdict materializes an empty bucket for every distinct client key seen (including denied/first-touch requests), so unique forged X-Forwarded-For values grow memory unbounded between sweep() calls — flag the attacker-controlled-key DoS framing to critic-security.

sweep() relies on any(t >= cutoff) but check() only prunes lazily on access, so quiet clients accumulate fully-stale lists that sweep must scan end-to-end (no short-circuit), making each sweep O(total retained timestamps) rather than O(buckets).

current_usage() does a full O(n) linear scan of the per-key list on every call and never prunes, so the cost grows with un-pruned stale entries for clients that call this without going through check().
len(hits) <= self.limit followed by append admits limit+1 requests per window (off-by-one) — not a performance concern; defer detail to critic-logic.
